primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivables for hedge accounting of foreign exchange risk at consolidated level

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 6.3.5 restricts hedged items to those with external parties at consolidated level; paragraph 6.3.6 provides an exception for intragroup monetary items where FX differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation

supporting_authority.reason: Defines treatment of dividends on financial liabilities vs equity instruments, relevant for understanding the nature of dividend payables/receivables

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses net investment hedge accounting but does not specifically address intragroup dividend hedges
General hedge accounting documentation requirements but does not address core eligibility of intragroup items
authority_classification.reason: Addresses measurement of dividend payables but does not address hedge accounting eligibility for FX risk on dividends

treatment_families.family: External party requirement for hedged items at consolidated level
Exception for intragroup monetary items with non-eliminated FX exposure

approaches.label: Formal hedge accounting excluded for intragroup dividends at consolidated level
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 requires hedged items to involve parties external to the reporting entity; intragroup dividends are eliminated on consolidation, making them ineligible for hedge accounting at consolidated level unless the paragraph 6.3.6 exception applies

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item exception applies
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 permits hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items where FX differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation, which could theoretically apply if dividend receivables qualify as monetary items and FX differences survive consolidation
